data.narrative:
Auterion, a Series B-stage defense and security software company founded by Lorenz Meier and Kevin Sartori, has raised $130M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms in the same sector and stage, including Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Code Metal, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised ($130M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical for Series B-to-next-stage advancement, yields $247M. Peer-median analysis across 7 same-sector, same-stage comparables establishes a median valuation of $1.0B, with a range spanning $300M to $3.0B. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneous scale and market positioning within the Defense & Security peer set.

Synthesizing both methods produces a triangulated range of $210M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $624M. The stage-progression method anchors the lower bound, while peer comparables—particularly the median and upper quartile—support a substantially higher valuation. Key uncertainties include Auterion's relative competitive positioning within the peer set, the applicability of early-stage funding multiples given defense sector dynamics, and near-term capital requirements. Further diligence on customer concentration, contract backlog, and platform differentiation would narrow this range.
